words.definitionEn: a written or printed symbol that is used as a sign of something, for example the quality of something or who made or owns it
words.exampleEn: punctuation marks

words.definitionEn: to write or draw a symbol, line, etc. on something in order to give information about it
words.exampleEn: mark something, The flood level is marked by a white line on the wall.

words.definitionEn: to become the husband or wife of somebody; to get married to somebody
words.exampleEn: marry (somebody), She married a German.

words.definitionEn: a substance that things can be made from
words.exampleEn: building materials (= bricks, sand, glass, etc.)

words.definitionEn: the science of numbers and shapes. Branches of mathematics include arithmetic, algebra, geometry and trigonometry.
words.exampleEn: the school mathematics curriculum

words.definitionEn: mathematics, especially as a subject in school
words.exampleEn: The core subjects are English, maths and science.

words.definitionEn: used (to ask) if somebody is upset, unhappy, etc. or if there is a problem
words.exampleEn: What's the matter? Is there something wrong?

words.definitionEn: to be important or have an important effect on somebody/something
words.exampleEn: ‘I'm afraid I forgot that book again.’ ‘It doesn't matter (= it is not important enough to worry about).’

words.definitionEn: used to say that something is possible
words.exampleEn: That may or may not be true.

words.definitionEn: the main ways that large numbers of people receive information and entertainment, that is television, radio, newspapers and the internet
words.exampleEn: the national/international media

words.definitionEn: connected with illness and injury and their treatment
words.exampleEn: medical care/treatment

words.definitionEn: the study and treatment of diseases and injuries
words.exampleEn: advances in modern medicine

words.definitionEn: used when you are comparing two people or things, or two situations
words.exampleEn: You're as tall as your father.

words.definitionEn: your ability to remember things
words.exampleEn: memory for something, I have a bad memory for names.

words.definitionEn: while something else is happening
words.exampleEn: He sat watching her as she got ready.

words.definitionEn: to write or speak about something/somebody, especially without giving much information
words.exampleEn: mention something/somebody, Sorry, I won't mention it again.

words.definitionEn: a type of solid mineral substance that is usually hard and shiny and that heat and electricity can travel through, for example tin, iron and gold
words.exampleEn: a piece/sheet of metal

words.definitionEn: a particular way of doing something
words.exampleEn: Which method is the most effective?

words.definitionEn: in a position in the middle of an object, group of objects, people, etc.; between the beginning and the end of something
words.exampleEn: Pens are kept in the middle drawer.

words.definitionEn: the part of something that is at an equal distance from all its edges or sides; a point or a period of time between the beginning and the end of something
words.exampleEn: in the middle, a lake with an island in the middle

words.definitionEn: used when showing that something is or was possible
words.exampleEn: He might get there in time, but I can't be sure.

words.definitionEn: the part of a person that makes them able to be aware of things, to think and to feel
words.exampleEn: the conscious/subconscious mind

words.definitionEn: to be upset, annoyed or worried by something
words.exampleEn: mind (something), I don't mind the cold—it's the rain I don't like.

words.definitionEn: of or belonging to the person writing or speaking
words.exampleEn: That's mine.

words.definitionEn: a piece of special flat glass that reflects images, so that you can see yourself when you look in it
words.exampleEn: in the mirror, He looked at himself in the mirror.

words.definitionEn: that cannot be found or that is not in its usual place; that has been removed, lost or destroyed
words.exampleEn: I never found the missing piece.

words.definitionEn: sleeping
words.exampleEn: I waited until they were all fast asleep (= sleeping deeply).

words.definitionEn: connected with mobile phones, tablets, etc.
words.exampleEn: What's your mobile number?

words.definitionEn: a mobile phone
words.exampleEn: Call me on my mobile.

words.definitionEn: an animal with a long tail, that climbs trees and lives in hot countries. There are several types of monkey and they are related to apes and humans.
words.exampleEn: Like humans, apes and monkeys live in complex social groupings.

words.definitionEn: the round object that moves around the earth once every 27½ days and shines at night by light reflected from the sun
words.exampleEn: the surface of the moon

words.definitionEn: mainly; generally
words.exampleEn: The sauce is mostly cream.

words.definitionEn: a road vehicle with two wheels, driven by an engine, with one seat for the driver and often a seat for a passenger behind the driver
words.exampleEn: He never learned to ride a motorcycle or to drive a car.

words.definitionEn: an act of moving the body or part of the body
words.exampleEn: hand/eye movements

words.definitionEn: connected with music; containing music
words.exampleEn: musical styles/tastes

words.definitionEn: a person who plays a musical instrument or writes music, especially as a job
words.exampleEn: a jazz/rock/classical musician

words.definitionEn: used when the speaker or writer is also the person affected by an action
words.exampleEn: I cut myself on a knife.

words.definitionEn: measuring a short distance from one side to the other, especially in relation to length
words.exampleEn: Stray dogs wander the steep narrow lanes of the old town.

words.definitionEn: connected with a particular nation; shared by a whole nation
words.exampleEn: Decide whether it would be better to advertise in a national or a local newspaper.

words.definitionEn: all the plants, animals and things that exist in the universe that are not made by people
words.exampleEn: Take time to appreciate the beauties of nature.

words.definitionEn: almost; not quite; not completely
words.exampleEn: The bottle's nearly empty.

words.definitionEn: that is needed for a purpose or a reason
words.exampleEn: necessary to do something, It may be necessary to buy a new one.

words.definitionEn: the part of the body between the head and the shoulders
words.exampleEn: He tied a scarf around his neck.

words.definitionEn: a situation when something is necessary or must be done
words.exampleEn: to satisfy/meet/fulfil a need

words.definitionEn: not one nor the other of two things or people
words.exampleEn: Neither answer is correct.

words.definitionEn: not one nor the other of two things or people
words.exampleEn: Neither answer is correct.

words.definitionEn: anxious about something or afraid of something
words.exampleEn: I felt really nervous before the interview.

words.definitionEn: a complicated system of roads, lines, tubes, nerves, etc. that are connected to each other and operate together
words.exampleEn: a rail/road/canal network

words.definitionEn: a sound, especially when it is loud, unpleasant or frightening
words.exampleEn: There was a rattling noise coming from the back of the car.

words.definitionEn: making a lot of noise
words.exampleEn: noisy children/traffic/crowds

words.definitionEn: not one of a group of people or things; not any
words.exampleEn: None of these pens works/work.

words.definitionEn: having a rank below that of a senior person and helping them in their work
words.exampleEn: the assistant manager/director/commissioner

words.definitionEn: typical, usual or ordinary; what you would expect
words.exampleEn: quite/perfectly (= completely) normal

words.definitionEn: usually; in normal circumstances
words.exampleEn: I would never normally discuss this.

words.definitionEn: a person who helps or supports somebody, usually in their job
words.exampleEn: My assistant will now demonstrate the machine in action.

words.definitionEn: a sheet of paper giving written or printed information, usually put in a public place
words.exampleEn: There was a notice on the board saying the class had been cancelled.

words.definitionEn: to see or hear somebody/something; to become aware of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: People were making fun of him but he didn't seem to notice.

words.definitionEn: a story long enough to fill a complete book, in which the characters and events are usually imaginary
words.exampleEn: to write/publish/read a novel

words.definitionEn: not in or to any place
words.exampleEn: I had nowhere to live, so I was sleeping on my sister's couch.

words.definitionEn: to give a number to something as part of a series or list
words.exampleEn: number something, All the seats in the stadium are numbered.

words.definitionEn: a small hard fruit with a very hard shell that grows on some trees
words.exampleEn: to crack a nut (= open it)

words.definitionEn: the mass of salt water that covers most of the earth’s surface
words.exampleEn: The ship was dredged from the depths of the ocean.

words.definitionEn: an act of saying that you are willing to do something for somebody or give something to somebody
words.exampleEn: to receive a job offer

words.definitionEn: to say that you are willing to give something to somebody
words.exampleEn: offer (something), Josie had offered her services as a guide.

words.definitionEn: a person who is in a position of authority in the armed forces
words.exampleEn: army/military/naval officers

words.definitionEn: a thick liquid that is found in rock underground
words.exampleEn: Several companies are drilling for oil in the region.

words.definitionEn: used with verbs to express movement on or to a particular place or position
words.exampleEn: Move the books onto the second shelf.

words.definitionEn: a time when a particular situation makes it possible to do or achieve something
words.exampleEn: Don't miss this opportunity!

words.definitionEn: a person who competes in sports
words.exampleEn: Olympic athletes

words.definitionEn: something that you can choose to have or do; the freedom to choose what you do
words.exampleEn: As I see it, we have two options…

words.definitionEn: not unusual or different in any way
words.exampleEn: an ordinary sort of day

words.definitionEn: a group of people who form a business, club, etc. together in order to achieve a particular aim
words.exampleEn: He's the president of a large international organization.

words.definitionEn: to make all the arrangements for something to happen or be provided
words.exampleEn: He helped to organize various events.

words.definitionEn: existing at the beginning of a particular period, process or activity
words.exampleEn: The room still has many of its original features.

words.definitionEn: the reflexive form of we; used when you and another person or other people together cause and are affected by an action
words.exampleEn: We shouldn't blame ourselves for what happened.

words.definitionEn: an act of using violence to try to hurt or kill somebody
words.exampleEn: Five people were killed in the attacks that took place last night.

words.definitionEn: of, on or facing the outer side
words.exampleEn: The outside walls are damp.

words.definitionEn: the outer side or surface of something
words.exampleEn: The outside of the house needs painting.

words.definitionEn: on or to a place on the outside of something
words.exampleEn: You can park your car outside our house.

words.definitionEn: the part of a cooker that is like a box with a door on the front, in which food is cooked or heated
words.exampleEn: Take the cake out of the oven.

words.definitionEn: to use violence to try to hurt or kill somebody
words.exampleEn: Most dogs will not attack unless provoked.

words.definitionEn: to have something that belongs to you, especially because you have bought it
words.exampleEn: Do you own your house or do you rent it?

words.definitionEn: a person who owns something
words.exampleEn: a dog/pet/gun owner

words.definitionEn: to put clothes, etc. into a bag in preparation for a trip away from home
words.exampleEn: I haven't packed yet.

words.definitionEn: the feelings that you have in your body when you have been hurt or when you are ill
words.exampleEn: a cry of pain

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is painting buildings, walls, etc.
words.exampleEn: He works as a painter and decorator.

words.definitionEn: the official home of a king, queen, president, etc.
words.exampleEn: Buckingham Palace

words.definitionEn: to be present at an event
words.exampleEn: We'd like as many people as possible to attend.

words.definitionEn: underpants or knickers
words.exampleEn: a pair of pants

words.definitionEn: the act of stopping a vehicle at a place and leaving it there for a period of time
words.exampleEn: There is no parking here between 9 a.m. and 6 p.m.

words.definitionEn: used for asking people to listen to something that is being announced
words.exampleEn: Attention, please! Passengers for flight KL412 are requested to go to gate 21 immediately.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize that you are referring to one individual person, thing or type of thing and not others
words.exampleEn: There is one particular patient I'd like you to see.

words.definitionEn: the act of listening to, looking at or thinking about something/somebody carefully; interest that people show in somebody/something
words.exampleEn: the report’s attention to detail

words.definitionEn: to achieve the required standard in an exam, a test, etc.
words.exampleEn: I'm not really expecting to pass first time.

words.definitionEn: a person who is travelling in a car, bus, train, plane or ship and who is not driving it or working on it
words.exampleEn: airline/cruise/rail/bus passengers

words.definitionEn: from one side of something to the other
words.exampleEn: She smiled at me as she walked past.

words.definitionEn: a person who is receiving medical treatment, especially in a hospital
words.exampleEn: cancer/AIDS/heart patients

words.definitionEn: the regular way in which something happens or is done
words.exampleEn: changing patterns of urban life

words.definitionEn: the money that somebody gets for doing regular work
words.exampleEn: Her job is hard work, but the pay is good.

words.definitionEn: a situation or a period of time in which there is no war or violence in a country or an area
words.exampleEn: world peace

words.definitionEn: a small British coin and unit of money. There are 100 pence in one pound (£1).
words.exampleEn: He had a few pennies in his pocket.

words.definitionEn: used to express the cost or amount of something for each person, number used, distance travelled, etc.
words.exampleEn: Rooms cost £50 per person, per night.

words.definitionEn: by, in or for every hundred
words.exampleEn: a 15 per cent rise in price

words.definitionEn: by, in or for every hundred
words.exampleEn: a 15 per cent rise in price

words.definitionEn: one part in every hundred
words.exampleEn: per cent of something/somebody, Poor families spend about 80 to 90 per cent of their income on food.

words.definitionEn: pleasant to look at, especially in a sexual way
words.exampleEn: an attractive woman

words.definitionEn: to entertain an audience by playing a piece of music, acting in a play, etc.
words.exampleEn: to perform a song/dance/play

words.definitionEn: possibly
words.exampleEn: ‘Are you going to come?’ ‘Perhaps. I'll see how I feel.’

words.definitionEn: the act of allowing somebody to do something, especially when this is done by somebody in a position of authority
words.exampleEn: permission for something, You must ask permission for all major expenditure.

words.definitionEn: the various aspects of a person’s character that combine to make them different from other people
words.exampleEn: His wife has a strong personality.

words.definitionEn: an animal, a bird, etc. that you have at home for pleasure, rather than one that is kept for work or food
words.exampleEn: Do you have any pets?

words.definitionEn: a liquid obtained from petroleum, used as fuel in car engines, etc.
words.exampleEn: to fill a car up with petrol

words.definitionEn: to take a photograph of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: photograph somebody/something, a workshop on photographing wildlife

words.definitionEn: connected with a person’s body rather than their mind
words.exampleEn: physical activity/fitness

words.definitionEn: the scientific study of matter and energy and the relationships between them, including the study of forces, heat, light, sound, electricity and the structure of atoms
words.exampleEn: a degree in physics

words.definitionEn: the group of people who have gathered to watch or listen to something (a play, concert, somebody speaking, etc.)
words.exampleEn: The audience was/were clapping for 10 minutes.

words.definitionEn: to choose somebody/something from a group of people or things
words.exampleEn: pick somebody/something, Pick a number from one to twenty.

words.definitionEn: a person who operates the controls of an aircraft, especially as a job
words.exampleEn: an airline pilot

words.definitionEn: a large round object in space that moves around a star (such as the sun) and receives light from it
words.exampleEn: the planets of our solar system

words.definitionEn: to put plants, seeds, etc. in the ground to grow
words.exampleEn: to plant a tree/seed/crop

words.definitionEn: made of plastic
words.exampleEn: a plastic bag/bottle/cup

words.definitionEn: a light strong material that is produced by chemical processes and can be formed into shapes when heated. There are many different types of plastic, used to make different objects and fabrics.
words.exampleEn: The pipes should be made of plastic.

words.definitionEn: a flat, usually round, dish that you put food on
words.exampleEn: sandwiches on a plate

words.definitionEn: the raised flat area next to the track at a train station where you get on or off the train
words.exampleEn: (British English), What platform does it go from?

words.definitionEn: to make somebody happy
words.exampleEn: please somebody, You can't please everybody.

words.definitionEn: feeling happy about something
words.exampleEn: You're coming? I'm so pleased.

words.definitionEn: a person who writes books or the person who wrote a particular book
words.exampleEn: Who is your favourite author?

words.definitionEn: a small piece of material like a small bag sewn into or onto a piece of clothing so that you can carry things in it
words.exampleEn: a jacket/coat/shirt/trouser pocket

words.definitionEn: having or showing good manners and respect for the feelings of others
words.exampleEn: polite to somebody, Please be polite to our guests.

words.definitionEn: the process of making air, water, soil, etc. dirty; the state of being dirty
words.exampleEn: air/water pollution

words.definitionEn: connected with modern popular music
words.exampleEn: a pop song

words.definitionEn: popular music of the sort that has been popular since the 1950s, usually with a strong rhythm and simple tunes, often contrasted with rock, soul and other forms of popular music
words.exampleEn: rock, pop and soul

words.definitionEn: all the people who live in a particular area, city or country; the total number of people who live there
words.exampleEn: One third of the world’s population consumes/consume two thirds of the world’s resources.

words.definitionEn: the place where somebody/something is located
words.exampleEn: Where would be the best position for the lights?

words.definitionEn: something that you own or have with you at a particular time
words.exampleEn: Prisoners were allowed no personal possessions.
